Acta v0.2.0-pilot record · Generated from accepted canonical state (learning/url-as-state.md) after explicit acceptance (chat 2026-07-23 — session export pasted and confirmed after retrieval practice) · Never generated from browser state.

quiz record · ACCEPTED

URL-as-state — session 2: 3/4, review history traversal

Second session on the URL-as-state pattern: lessons on single-source state, the purity gradient, and history entry routes; retrieval practice scored 3/4 with the bfcache entry route as the waypoint to review next session.

Session result

3 / 4retrieval score
3waypoints covered
1to review next session

Second session on the URL-as-state pattern: lessons on single-source state, the purity gradient, and history entry routes; retrieval practice scored 3/4 with the bfcache entry route as the waypoint to review next session.

Reflection

The entry-routes framing finally made bfcache feel structural instead of trivia; the miss confirms it needs one more pass.

Review next session

03 · History traversal is not a load

A user presses back and the page shows the old list under the new URL. First suspect?

Cache restores resurrect the old DOM without re-running the glue — the diagnosed cause, and the entry route designs forget first.

Waypoints this session

#WaypointGrounding
01The URL is the only stateE1E4
02Pure core, effectful edgeE1
03History traversal is not a loadE2E3

Sources

  1. [E1] feature-xray record — tag filtering, verified
  2. [E2] find-the-cause record — bfcache diagnosis
  3. [E3] MDN — History API and pageshow
  4. [E4] spec.md — tag-filter scope

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
